name: Fish Biology and Ecology Studies
description: This cluster of papers focuses on the length-weight relationships, morphology, biodiversity, and biogeography of various fish species, with an emphasis on freshwater ecosystems. It also explores genetic diversity, conservation, and the influence of environmental factors on fish populations.

name: Aquaculture Nutrition and Growth
description: This cluster of papers focuses on the metabolism and nutrition of aquaculture feeds, with an emphasis on sustainable sources of nutrients such as fish meal and fish oil replacements, utilization of plant proteins, carbohydrates, and biofloc technology. It also explores the impact of different dietary components on fish health and growth performance.

name: Echinoderm biology and ecology
description: This cluster of papers explores the high-value components, bioactives, aquaculture, and pharmacological potential of sea cucumbers, focusing on triterpene glycosides and their biological activities. It also delves into regeneration, sustainable management, and the use of sea cucumbers in functional foods.

name: Aquatic life and conservation
description: This cluster of papers focuses on the life history, ecology, conservation, and management of seahorses, including topics such as aquaculture, genome evolution, male pregnancy, habitat preferences, reproductive strategies, and the impact of trade on seahorse populations.

name: Seaweed-derived Bioactive Compounds
description: This cluster of papers focuses on the bioactive compounds found in seaweed, particularly their functional food applications and potential health benefits. It covers a wide range of topics including antioxidant activity, sulfated polysaccharides, fucoidan, and the nutritional value of marine algae.

name: Engineering and Agricultural Innovations
description: This cluster of papers focuses on water resource management, irrigation techniques, renewable energy applications, soil salinity assessment, crop monitoring, and the impact of climate change on agricultural practices. It also addresses energy consumption in agricultural and industrial sectors, as well as the use of GIS and remote sensing technologies for water and land management.

name: Innovations in Aquaponics and Hydroponics Systems
description: This cluster of papers explores the challenges and opportunities in aquaponic systems, focusing on sustainability, nutrient recycling, commercial production, water quality, fish welfare, urban agriculture, microbial communities, and IoT monitoring. The research covers topics such as the integration of aquaculture and hydroponics, nutrient management, economic feasibility, and the potential for aquaponics to address global food challenges.
